x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces DOC's open accountability data (people in custody, facilities, daily population/security indicators, incidents) and adds schedule_visit and file_complaint — the net-new write workflows that today have no digital contract.

server.name: io.nyc.doc
server.title: NYC Department of Correction (DOC)
server.description: Agent-native access to the New York City Department of Correction — people in custody (the daily in-custody snapshot and the live P.I.C. lookup), facilities, daily population and Local Law 33/85 security indicators, and safety/security incidents from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to schedule a visit and file a complaint or records request that today have no public API.
server.instructions: Use find_people_in_custody / get_person_in_custody to search the daily in-custody population or look up a person by NYSID, Book & Case number, or DOC inmate id (the live Inmate Lookup / 'P.I.C. Lookup'). find_facilities lists DOC jails (mostly on Rikers Island). find_daily_population answers population and security-indicator questions (Local Law 33 rates per 100 ADP, Article 730 waitlist, medical non-production). find_incidents covers deaths, slashings/stabbings, fights, assaults on staff, staff injuries, and lock-ins. schedule_visit (net-new) requests a visit with a person in custody and mutates state — confirm the person, facility, date, and visitor identity with the user before submitting. file_complaint (net-new) files a complaint, grievance, or records/FOIL request and also mutates state — confirm the type, subject, and description first. Handle all person-in-custody data as sensitive.

tools.name: find_people_in_custody
tools.title: Find People In Custody
tools.description: Search the daily in-custody population, or the live P.I.C. lookup by NYSID / Book & Case number, filtered by custody level or facility.

tools.name: get_person_in_custody
tools.title: Get Person In Custody
tools.description: Fetch a person in DOC custody (demographics, custody level, top charge, facility, admission/discharge) by DOC inmate id.

tools.name: find_facilities
tools.title: Find Facilities
tools.description: List DOC facilities (jails), including the Rikers Island and borough detention centers, by borough, type, or Rikers Island status.

tools.name: find_daily_population
tools.title: Find Daily Population
tools.description: Search DOC daily population and security-indicator metrics (Local Law 33 rates per 100 ADP, average daily population, Article 730 waitlist, medical non-production) by metric, facility, or year.

tools.name: find_incidents
tools.title: Find Incidents
tools.description: Search DOC safety/security incidents — deaths, slashings/stabbings, fights, assaults on staff, staff injuries, and emergency lock-ins — by type, facility, or date range.

tools.name: list_my_visits
tools.title: List My Visits
tools.description: List the authenticated visitor's own scheduled visits and their status — today arranged only offline or through vendor portals.

tools.name: schedule_visit
tools.title: Schedule a Visit
tools.description: Request a visit with a person in DOC custody (report the person by NYSID, Book & Case number, or inmate id). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — visits are arranged by phone, in person, or through vendor portals. Requires an authenticated visitor. Mutates state — confirm the person, facility, date, and visitor identity first.

tools.name: file_complaint
tools.title: File a Complaint or Records Request
tools.description: File a complaint, grievance, or records/FOIL request with DOC and receive a tracking number. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — filed only by phone, mail, in person, or the citywide OpenRecords portal. Requires an authenticated user. Mutates state — confirm the type, subject, and description first.
